text.title: MCP Worked Example — Weather + DB Server
text.summary: End-to-end worked example: ship a Python MCP server exposing a weather lookup and a read-only DB query, registered in Claude Code via stdio.

section.title: Brief
section.p: Internal data team wants Claude Code to access (a) current weather for a city for a logistics-planning agent, and (b) a read-only SELECT against an analytics postgres replica. One developer, one workstation, stdio is fine.

section.title: STEP 1 — Normalized tool list
section.code: - name: ping description: Health probe; returns server status and version inputs: {} - name: weather.get_current description: Get current weather conditions for a city inputs: city: {type: string, required: true, description: "City name in English, e.g. 'Porto'"} - name: db.run_query description: Run a read-only SQL SELECT against analytics replica; returns ≤500 rows inputs: sql: {type: string, required: true, description: "SELECT statement; INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE rejected"}

section.title: STEP 2 — Transport choice
section.p: One client (Claude Code on same workstation), no cross-host traffic, no concurrent users → stdio. Auth.mode = none.

section.title: STEP 4 — Server scaffold (excerpt)
STEP 5 — Smoke test trace
section.code: $ npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector python server.py [handshake] negotiated spec 2026-07-28 [list_tools] 3 tools: ping, weather.get_current, db.run_query [call_tool] ping → {"server_version":"1.0.0","payload":{"status":"ok"},"error":null} ✓ 28 ms [call_tool] weather.get_current {"city":"Porto"} → 22°C, clear ✓ 41 ms [call_tool] db.run_query {"sql":"DROP TABLE x"} → error.forbidden ✓ guard works
